task_bootstrap: Use $task-framer, $long-horizon-worker, and $task-handoff-state to continue {task_ref}, turn the objective into concrete milestones and validation, and keep working until the first dependency-safe slice is implemented or a real blocker appears.
resume.single: Use $long-horizon-worker and $task-handoff-state to continue {task_ref} from the current handoff and validate the next slice before stopping.
resume.rolling.bounded: Use $task-framer, $course-corrector, $long-horizon-worker, $task-evaluator, $task-handoff-state, and $thread-monitor-sidecar to continue {task_ref}. First start or reuse exactly one visible observer-only monitor sidecar subagent for this thread, point it at task slug `{task_slug}` using `{monitor_loop_command}`, and retarget that same sidecar if the active task changes later in the thread. Keep planning bounded, auto-advance through queued milestones, replenish the queue when policy allows and budget remains, do not self-pause before the configured minimum runtime and milestone floor unless a blocker or decision boundary appears, and stop only on a real blocker, decision boundary, budget limit, or the configured run budget.
resume.rolling.until_stopped: Use $task-framer, $course-corrector, $long-horizon-worker, $task-evaluator, $task-handoff-state, and $thread-monitor-sidecar to continue {task_ref}. First start or reuse exactly one visible observer-only monitor sidecar subagent for this thread, point it at task slug `{task_slug}` using `{monitor_loop_command}`, and retarget that same sidecar if the active task changes later in the thread. Keep planning bounded, auto-advance through queued milestones, replenish the queue when policy allows, and keep going until a real blocker, decision boundary, safe-work exhaustion, or an external manual pause appears. Do not impose an internal runtime budget or milestone cap.
verifier_sweep: Use $task-evaluator and $task-handoff-state to inspect {task_ref}, rerun the strongest validation listed in runbook.md, refresh findings or artifacts only if evidence changed, and stop after updating task state with the current evaluation result.
backlog_refresh: Use $task-framer, $course-corrector, and $task-handoff-state to review {task_ref}, tighten the next 2 to 3 milestones, refresh execution policy if needed, preserve prior progress, and leave a new handoff without broad implementation unless the new next slice is obvious and dependency-safe.
retrospective: Use $task-retrospector and $task-handoff-state to inspect {task_ref}, review checkpoints.json, handoff.md, progress.md, artifacts.json, and the relevant findings, write a short retrospective note with the top reusable lessons, record a structured improvement candidate only when the next action is concrete, and update task state only if the retrospective changes the next queued work.
benchmark: Use $task-evaluator, $task-retrospector, and $task-handoff-state to inspect {benchmark_root} in this workspace, run the strongest relevant benchmark validation or scoring command, summarize the evidence for success, state fidelity, resume fidelity, replan quality, and validation quality, and record follow-up candidates only when the result justifies them.
improvement_promotion: Use $task-framer, $long-horizon-worker, $task-evaluator, and $task-handoff-state to continue {task_ref} as the implementation path for promoted improvement candidate {candidate_ref}. Restate the candidate source, turn it into concrete milestones and validation, keep the work local-first plus verifier-backed, and stop only after the next safe slice is implemented and evaluated or a real blocker appears.
